description: The Gillard government introduced a carbon pricing mechanism in Australia in 2012, which quickly became the central issue of the 2013 federal election. Tony Abbott's 'Coalition' campaigned on a platform to 'axe the tax,' leading to a landslide victory and the eventual repeal of the policy in 2014.

key_events.description: Tony Abbott officially launches the 'Axe the Tax' campaign nationwide.
Rising energy prices lead to widespread media headlines blaming the carbon pricing mechanism.
The 2013 federal election results confirm a mandate to remove the carbon tax.
The Abbott government introduces the Carbon Tax Repeal Bill in Parliament.

calibration_notes: Ideal for calibrating public response to polarizing political messaging and election-driven policy reversals.
